name: Reginald Veal
abstract: Reginald Veal (born November 5, 1963) is an American jazz bassist and multi-instrumentalist from New Orleans, Louisiana. Veal grew up in New Orleans where he began piano lessons at a very early age.

works.t: We Three Kings of Orient Are

about_quotes.speaker: Ellis Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: I had Victor Goines was playing tenor and Noel Kendricks and Reginald Veal was playing bass... So we got off the bandstand
about_quotes.doc_title: Ellis Marsalis interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Orleans, Louisiana, January 7, 2020

about_quotes.speaker: Ellis Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: I used to work on Wednesday nights with a bass player – wonderful young bass player at the time – named Reginald Veal
about_quotes.doc_title: Ellis Marsalis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jason Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: I think it was either in that moment or sometime – it might have been at that moment I thought, this is interesting. Here we are at the end of the decade. Wynton’s got this new music with his band with Herlin Riley and Reginald Veal and etc.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jason Marsalis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bruce Johnstone
about_quotes.evidence: Michael was brilliant. He had Joey Calderazzo on piano, Reginald Veal on bass, and Jeff Watts on drums.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bruce Johnstone interviewed by Monk Rowe, Dunkirk, New York, October 11, 2013
